key_insight: The paper presents a unified analytical loss model that diagnoses photoelectrochemical cell performance and turns those losses into rational design rules for both built-in junction and semiconductor-electrolyte junction devices.
engineering_relevance: It gives engineers a quantitative way to pinpoint where efficiency is lost in real PEC electrodes and to select targeted fixes such as improved light management, reduced recombination, or better interfacial catalysis.
method: Unified framework for BIJ and SEJ photoelectrodes using physically meaningful parameters
Generates current-voltage curves, efficiency maps, and Sankey energy-flow diagrams
Fits experimental J-V data to decompose losses into thermodynamic, optical, recombination, and interfacial terms
evidence_signal: The framework is validated against literature data spanning solar water splitting, CO2 reduction, NH3 synthesis, and solar redox flow batteries, and it is used to compare absorber classes such as Si, perovskites, hematite, and BiVO4.
watch_item: The model’s diagnostic value depends on reliable parameter fitting and may be sensitive to assumptions used to map real device behavior into the unified analytical framework.
paper_title: From Loss Diagnosis to Rational Design: A Unified Analytical Model for Photoelectrochemical Cells
